notes: Thirty researched markets spanning meteor showers, solar activity, space missions, comets, scientific discoveries, and astronomy-adjacent weather. initial_yes_pct drives the on-chain seed YES/NO pool ratio (YES=pct, NO=100-pct Stars).

markets.title: Lyrids peak ZHR exceeds 18 meteors/hour
markets.resolution_source: International Meteor Organization live ZHR graph at imo.net
markets.YES_condition: IMO live ZHR for shower code LYR reaches 18 or higher in any 1-hour bin between 2026-04-21 18:00Z and 2026-04-22 06:00Z.
markets.why_interesting: The Lyrids (parent: Comet Thatcher) peak April 22 with historical ZHR 18. 2026 moon is minimal — conditions favor a classical peak.
markets.ui_description: Historical ZHR is exactly 18, but observed rates often fall below due to weather and light pollution. 2026 has favorable moon conditions (27% waxing crescent sets before peak). Slight YES edge — the bar is set precisely at the long-run mean.
markets.analysis: Historical ZHR = 18. Favorable moon. Weather is the swing factor. Slight YES edge.

markets.title: Eta Aquariids peak ZHR exceeds 40 meteors/hour
markets.resolution_source: International Meteor Organization live ZHR graph at imo.net
markets.YES_condition: IMO live ZHR for shower code ETA reaches 40 or higher in any 1-hour bin between 2026-05-05 18:00Z and 2026-05-06 12:00Z.
markets.why_interesting: Eta Aquariids (parent: Halley's Comet) peak May 5–6. Historical ZHR ~50 but 2026 peak coincides with an 84% illuminated moon.
markets.ui_description: ZHR measures corrected rate, not visual rate — so a bright moon washes out fainter trails but the corrected number can still cross 40. Halley's debris stream is one of the most reliable in the calendar. YES favored with moon-related uncertainty in the measurement.
markets.analysis: Corrected ZHR not visual rate. Halley stream is reliable. YES favored.

markets.title: Perseids 2026 peak ZHR exceeds 100
markets.resolution_source: International Meteor Organization live ZHR graph at imo.net
markets.YES_condition: IMO live ZHR for shower code PER reaches 100 or higher in any 1-hour bin between 2026-08-12 18:00Z and 2026-08-13 12:00Z.
markets.why_interesting: The Perseids peak August 12–13 with historical ZHR 100–150 and new moon in 2026 — close to ideal conditions.
markets.ui_description: Perseids are extremely reliable — the shower crosses ZHR 100 in the vast majority of recorded years. 2026's new moon removes the usual biggest risk. Only risk: an unusual low year, which is rare. Strong YES favored.
markets.analysis: Historically reliable at 100+. New moon 2026. YES strong.

markets.title: Fireball brighter than magnitude -8 recorded in April 2026
markets.resolution_source: American Meteor Society fireball database at amsmeteors.org
markets.YES_condition: The AMS fireball database logs at least one event with peak magnitude ≤-8 between 2026-04-01 00:00Z and 2026-04-30 23:59Z.
markets.why_interesting: Fireballs brighter than Venus (-4.6) are rare; brighter than -8 is exceptional. April has Lyrids plus the sporadic background.
markets.ui_description: AMS logs multiple bright fireballs each month. The Lyrids are known for producing fireballs, which adds lift. But -8 is extremely bright — roughly quarter-moon brightness compressed into a second. YES slightly favored, not a lock.
markets.analysis: AMS multi-fireball/month. Lyrids help. -8 is a high bar. YES slight.

markets.title: Unexpected meteor outburst (>3× predicted ZHR) from any shower before August 2026
markets.resolution_source: IMO alerts and MPC published outburst reports
markets.YES_condition: Any IMO-recognized annual shower registers an observed ZHR at least 3× its IMO-predicted peak ZHR in any 1-hour bin before 2026-08-01 00:00Z.
markets.why_interesting: Meteor outbursts are rare and genuinely unpredictable. Last memorable ones: Draconids 2011 (spectacular), Tau Herculids 2022 (disappointing).
markets.ui_description: Outbursts are rare — maybe 1–2 per decade for any given shower. The four-month window narrows the odds further. This is a clear NO by prior, but a pleasantly asymmetric YES bet if the stream surprises.
markets.analysis: ~1–2/decade per shower. NO strong. High-risk payoff.

markets.title: X-class solar flare occurs before May 15, 2026
markets.resolution_source: NOAA Space Weather Prediction Center GOES X-ray flare list
markets.YES_condition: At least one solar flare classified X1.0 or stronger appears in the NOAA SWPC GOES flare event list with peak time before 2026-05-15 00:00Z.
markets.why_interesting: Cycle 25 peaked in 2024 but remains active. X-class flares occur ~10–20 times per year near solar max.
markets.ui_description: With roughly 15 X-flares per year during the active cycle phase, the probability of at least one in any 25-day window is very high. Declining phase historically still produces big flares. Strong YES.
markets.analysis: ~15 X/year. 25-day window. YES strong.

markets.title: Kp index reaches 7+ (G3 strong storm) before June 1, 2026
markets.resolution_source: NOAA SWPC planetary Kp index feed
markets.YES_condition: NOAA SWPC reports a planetary Kp value of 7 or higher in any 3-hour bin before 2026-06-01 00:00Z.
markets.why_interesting: Kp 7 is a G3 Strong geomagnetic storm. Aurora visible at mid-latitudes. Multiple G3+ events hit in 2024–2025.
markets.ui_description: G3 storms occur ~12 times per solar cycle. In a ~42-day window during the declining-but-active phase, the base rate gives moderate probability. Recurrent coronal holes can trigger one even without a CME. YES favored, not certain.
markets.analysis: ~12/cycle. 42-day window. YES moderate.

markets.title: Aurora visible below 45°N latitude during April–May 2026
markets.resolution_source: SpaceWeatherLive.com geolocated sighting reports
markets.YES_condition: At least three independent observer reports (photo + location) placing aurora below 45°N latitude appear on SpaceWeatherLive or equivalent aggregator before 2026-06-01 00:00Z.
markets.why_interesting: In 2024–2025, aurora was visible in Florida (27°N) and southern Europe during extreme storms. Below 45°N requires Kp 7+.
markets.ui_description: Closely linked to the Kp 7+ market but requires additional conditions: clear skies, darkness, and someone actually out with a camera at mid-latitudes. Slightly lower probability than the pure Kp 7 market.
markets.analysis: Depends on Kp7 + clear mid-lat skies. Slight edge lower than Kp7.

markets.title: Sun produces an X5+ flare (extreme) before end of 2026
markets.resolution_source: NOAA Space Weather Prediction Center GOES X-ray flare list
markets.YES_condition: At least one solar flare classified X5.0 or stronger appears in the NOAA SWPC GOES flare event list with peak time before 2026-12-31 12:00Z.
markets.why_interesting: X5+ flares are among the most powerful solar events. Cycle 25 produced X9.0 in Oct 2024.
markets.ui_description: X5+ events fire roughly 1–3 times per solar cycle. Cycle 25 has already produced X9. The declining phase reduces but doesn't eliminate the probability through 2026. True 50/50 territory, priced slightly NO for caution.
markets.analysis: ~1–3/cycle. Cycle 25 declining. True coin flip.

markets.title: Solar radio flux (F10.7) drops below 100 sfu for 7 consecutive days before October 2026
markets.resolution_source: NOAA SWPC daily F10.7 observed flux
markets.YES_condition: NOAA SWPC observed F10.7 flux is below 100 sfu on seven consecutive calendar days at any point before 2026-10-01 00:00Z.
markets.why_interesting: F10.7 correlates with solar activity. Currently ~106 sfu. A sustained drop below 100 signals accelerating decline.
markets.ui_description: F10.7 has been hovering 100–130 through early 2026. Seven consecutive days below 100 requires significant cycle decline to materialize. Possible but not likely within five months. Slight NO.
markets.analysis: Currently near threshold. 7-day streak is the bar. Slight NO.

markets.title: Artemis II crew returns safely to Earth
markets.resolution_source: NASA official mission recovery announcement
markets.YES_condition: NASA confirms nominal splashdown and crew recovery for Artemis II mission before 2026-04-12 00:00Z.
markets.why_interesting: First crewed lunar flyby since Apollo 17 (1972). ~10-day mission.
markets.ui_description: RESOLVED YES. Artemis II splashed down successfully on April 11, 2026. Market retained for historical reference and audit trail.
markets.analysis: Resolved YES — mission successful.

markets.title: Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope launches before January 1, 2027
markets.resolution_source: NASA launch manifest and official post-launch announcement
markets.YES_condition: NASA confirms Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope reaches orbit before 2027-01-01 00:00Z.
markets.why_interesting: Flagship wide-field NIR telescope. Official target: by May 2027. Earliest credible date: fall 2026.
markets.ui_description: Fall 2026 is optimistic. NASA's track record with flagships suggests delays (JWST slipped 14 years, Europa Clipper on time). 'On track' language rarely holds through final integration. NO favored but upset possible.
markets.analysis: Flagship risk. NASA slippage precedent. NO favored.

markets.title: China's Chang'e 7 launches in 2026
markets.resolution_source: CNSA and Xinhua official launch confirmation
markets.YES_condition: CNSA confirms launch of Chang'e 7 before 2027-01-01 00:00Z.
markets.why_interesting: Lunar south-pole orbiter + lander + rover + hopper. China's lunar program has been notably punctual.
markets.ui_description: Chang'e 5 (2020) and Chang'e 6 (2024) both launched on or near schedule. Mid-2026 target is credible. YES favored.
markets.analysis: China's lunar program on-time record. YES favored.

markets.title: SpaceX Starship reaches full orbit before July 2026
markets.resolution_source: SpaceX and FAA launch license records plus post-flight telemetry
markets.YES_condition: SpaceX achieves orbital insertion of a Starship upper stage (at least one full orbit) before 2026-07-01 00:00Z.
markets.why_interesting: IFT-5 (Oct 2024) caught the booster. Orbital insertion with ship relight is the next milestone.
markets.ui_description: SpaceX iteration speed is fast, but orbital insertion with a working relight is a harder milestone than suborbital flights. 60/40 reflects optimism with realistic caution about integration timelines.
markets.analysis: Fast iteration vs. hard milestone. 60/40.

markets.title: ESA PLATO telescope launches before January 2027
markets.resolution_source: ESA official PLATO mission launch confirmation
markets.YES_condition: ESA confirms PLATO spacecraft successfully launched before 2027-01-01 00:00Z.
markets.why_interesting: PLATO hunts habitable-zone rocky planets around 200,000 stars. Target: Dec 2026 on Ariane 6.
markets.ui_description: December 2026 is ambitious for a flagship on a relatively new launcher. Ariane 6 flew first in 2024 and is still ramping cadence. Genuine 50/50 — one late-breaking schedule update could decide this.
markets.analysis: New launcher + flagship integration. 50/50.

markets.title: Comet C/2025 R3 (PANSTARRS) reaches naked-eye brightness (mag ≤ 6.0)
markets.resolution_source: COBS (cobs.si) and AAVSO magnitude estimates
markets.YES_condition: At least three independent COBS/AAVSO observer estimates place C/2025 R3 at total magnitude 6.0 or brighter before 2026-05-16 00:00Z.
markets.why_interesting: Comet C/2025 R3 is being tracked as a potential naked-eye candidate around April 2026.
markets.ui_description: Comet brightness predictions are famously unreliable. 'May become visible' language is standard hedging. History says more comets disappoint than deliver. NO slightly favored.
markets.analysis: Comets disappoint more than deliver. NO slight.

markets.title: New comet with perihelion inside Earth's orbit discovered in 2026
markets.resolution_source: Minor Planet Center circulars
markets.YES_condition: MPC publishes a new comet designation with computed perihelion distance < 1.0 AU before 2027-01-01 00:00Z.
markets.why_interesting: SOHO alone discovers ~30 comets/year. Vera Rubin Observatory 2026 ops will dramatically raise the discovery rate.
markets.ui_description: Between SOHO sungrazers and the Rubin LSST coming fully online, the discovery of at least one comet with perihelion inside 1 AU in 2026 is near-certain. Strong YES.
markets.analysis: SOHO + Rubin. Near-certain. Strong YES.

markets.title: Vera Rubin Observatory discovers a new near-Earth asteroid in first 30 days of operations
markets.resolution_source: Minor Planet Center and Rubin project announcements
markets.YES_condition: MPC assigns a NEA designation to at least one new object first detected by Rubin LSST within 30 days of its survey operations start date.
markets.why_interesting: Rubin's 8.4m mirror and 3.2 gigapixel camera scan the whole visible sky every ~3 nights. NEA discovery is a primary science goal.
markets.ui_description: Rubin is purpose-built for transient and small-body discovery. Finding at least one NEA in the first 30 nights is essentially guaranteed — the question is whether the 'first 30 days' is defined cleanly. YES heavily favored.
markets.analysis: Purpose-built for this. Near-certain. YES heavy.

markets.title: China's Tianwen-2 successfully collects Kamo'oalewa samples
markets.resolution_source: CNSA and Xinhua official mission status updates
markets.YES_condition: CNSA confirms successful sample collection from asteroid 469219 Kamo'oalewa before 2026-10-15 00:00Z.
markets.why_interesting: If successful, only the third asteroid sample return after Hayabusa and OSIRIS-REx.
markets.ui_description: China's deep-space track record is strong. Touch-and-go sample collection on a small, fast-rotating asteroid is technically hard but China has invested heavily. YES favored, genuine uncertainty.
markets.analysis: Strong program. Hard touch-and-go. YES moderate.

markets.title: Comet C/2026 A1 (MAPS) survives perihelion passage
markets.resolution_source: SOHO/LASCO coronagraph imagery and MPC post-perihelion recovery
markets.YES_condition: SOHO/LASCO or ground recovery observations confirm an intact coma and nucleus for C/2026 A1 at least 72 hours after perihelion.
markets.why_interesting: Sun-grazing comets frequently disintegrate. Survival depends on nucleus size and composition.
markets.ui_description: Most sun-grazers disintegrate. Without known nucleus size, the prior favors disintegration. NO favored — this is a classic asymmetric high-payoff YES bet if the comet is larger than it looks.
markets.analysis: Sun-grazers disintegrate more than they survive. NO favored.

markets.title: JWST detects a potential biosignature in an exoplanet atmosphere in 2026
markets.resolution_source: Peer-reviewed paper or NASA press conference announcement
markets.YES_condition: A peer-reviewed paper or official NASA announcement before 2027-01-15 00:00Z claims a JWST-detected biosignature candidate (e.g. O2+CH4, DMS) at ≥3σ.
markets.why_interesting: JWST has been characterizing exoplanet atmospheres since 2023. A confirmed biosignature would be the biggest astronomy discovery in history.
markets.ui_description: This would be epochal. JWST has detected CO2 and water vapor, but a confirmed biosignature is a much higher bar. Extremely unlikely in any given year but not zero. NO heavy — a clean moonshot bet.
markets.analysis: Epochal bar. Low prior. Moonshot NO-heavy.

markets.title: Nobel Prize in Physics 2026 awarded for cosmology or dark-energy work
markets.resolution_source: Official Nobel Foundation announcement
markets.YES_condition: The 2026 Nobel Prize in Physics citation names cosmology, dark energy, or large-scale structure as the primary discovery.
markets.why_interesting: DESI DR1 (2024) hinted at dynamical dark energy. DR2 (2026) should confirm or refute. The committee is conservative but an upset is possible.
markets.ui_description: Cosmology hasn't won since 2019 (Peebles). Dark energy work is maturing but Nobel prefers decades of verification. Low probability, unpredictable year-to-year.
markets.analysis: Nobel conservative. Cosmology overdue but unpredictable. Low.

markets.title: LIGO O5 detects a neutron-star merger with electromagnetic counterpart
markets.resolution_source: LIGO/Virgo/KAGRA GCN alerts and follow-up publication
markets.YES_condition: A LIGO O5 gravitational-wave NS-merger detection with confirmed EM counterpart is published or announced before 2027-02-01 00:00Z.
markets.why_interesting: O5 is slated for late 2026. O3 produced the historic GW170817 NS merger. O5 sensitivity upgrades push further out.
markets.ui_description: O5 is a late-2026 start — limited 2026 observing time. NS mergers with EM counterpart are rare (~1 per run). Low but real probability inside 2026 alone.
markets.analysis: Late start cuts window. ~1/run rate. Low probability.

markets.title: Gaia Data Release 4 (DR4) published before January 2027
markets.resolution_source: ESA Gaia project official DR4 release announcement
markets.YES_condition: ESA officially releases Gaia DR4 data products to the public before 2027-01-01 00:00Z.
markets.why_interesting: Gaia DR4 targets December 2026 with 500 TB of astrometric data for 2.8 billion sources. ESA data releases are historically on time.
markets.ui_description: ESA targets December 2026. Data processing pipeline scale is unprecedented (50× DR3). Possible slight delay but ESA generally delivers close to schedule. YES slightly favored.
markets.analysis: ESA on-time record. Pipeline scale is new. Slight YES.

markets.title: LaserSETI reports an anomalous optical transient requiring follow-up in 2026
markets.resolution_source: SETI Institute published report or press release
markets.YES_condition: The SETI Institute publicly reports at least one LaserSETI candidate transient warranting follow-up before 2027-01-15 00:00Z.
markets.why_interesting: LaserSETI's optical technosignature detection network is expanding. A 'candidate' is a lower bar than a confirmed signal.
markets.ui_description: LaserSETI is expanding rapidly with new stations. A 'candidate' event is a much lower bar than a confirmed signal — but even candidates are rare and usually reclassified as aircraft/satellites. Low probability.
markets.analysis: Network expanding. Candidate bar is low but still rare. Low.

markets.title: Tbilisi has 3+ consecutive clear nights in May 2026
markets.resolution_source: Open-Meteo historical weather API for 41.7151°N, 44.8271°E
markets.YES_condition: Open-Meteo historical hourly cloud cover stays below 20% across at least three consecutive nights (00:00–04:00 local) in May 2026.
markets.why_interesting: Clear-sky runs are the difference between hobbyist and serious astronomy nights. Tbilisi May typically has 12–15 clear nights clustered in runs.
markets.ui_description: May is generally favorable for Tbilisi stargazing. Three consecutive clear nights inside a 31-day month is very likely given long-run base rates. Strong YES.
markets.analysis: Good month. 3-of-31 clear run very likely. YES strong.

markets.title: August 12, 2026 total solar eclipse in Spain has <50% cloud cover at centerline
markets.resolution_source: AEMET (Spanish Met Agency) observations plus EUMETSAT satellite imagery
markets.YES_condition: AEMET station readings and satellite composites show mean cloud cover below 50% along the centerline at totality on 2026-08-12.
markets.why_interesting: Total solar eclipse crosses Spain on August 12, 2026 — a once-in-a-generation event for continental Europe.
markets.ui_description: August in Spain is one of the driest months globally. The eclipse falls in early evening local time; afternoon convection is possible but clear skies are heavily favored. YES.
markets.analysis: Driest month in Spain. Evening timing. YES favored.

markets.title: Clear sky over Tbilisi during Lyrids peak window
markets.resolution_source: Open-Meteo forecast and post-event actuals for 41.7151°N, 44.8271°E
markets.YES_condition: Open-Meteo mean cloud cover below 30% for Tbilisi between 2026-04-22 21:00 local and 2026-04-23 04:00 local.
markets.why_interesting: The prime Lyrids observation window. Tbilisi late-April weather is transitional — can be clear or stormy.
markets.ui_description: Genuine uncertainty — April weather in Tbilisi is notably unpredictable. A true 50/50 market that trades on rolling forecast updates.
markets.analysis: Transitional weather. True 50/50.

markets.title: A location in Georgia (country) records Bortle 2 or darker sky in 2026
markets.resolution_source: Globe at Night database or Stellar verified dark-sky submission
markets.YES_condition: At least one SQM or Globe at Night submission from within Georgia in 2026 reports a reading corresponding to Bortle 2 or darker.
markets.why_interesting: Georgia's mountains (Kazbegi, Svaneti) are strong Bortle 2 candidates. Requires an observer to submit a verified measurement.
markets.ui_description: Kazbegi and Svaneti are plausibly Bortle 2. The market resolves on someone actually going there with an SQM meter and submitting. YES favored if observers participate, which is the real swing factor.
markets.analysis: Geography favors. Depends on observer submissions.

markets.title: Perseid peak (Aug 12–13) has <25% cloud cover across 3+ European countries simultaneously
markets.resolution_source: Open-Meteo aggregate readings for a basket of EU capital cities
markets.YES_condition: Open-Meteo reports mean cloud cover below 25% at three or more of {Madrid, Rome, Athens, Istanbul, Lisbon, Sofia} during 2026-08-12 22:00Z–2026-08-13 03:00Z.
markets.why_interesting: Perseids peak at new moon 2026 — perfect astronomical conditions. Question is whether weather cooperates at continental scale.
markets.ui_description: August is typically clear across southern and eastern Europe. Three of six candidate capitals clearing simultaneously is well within normal August base rates. YES favored.
markets.analysis: Dry August in southern/eastern EU. 3-of-6 likely. YES.
